title: Mbozi District Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2001
title_en: Mbozi District Council (Council Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2001

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This instrument may be cited as the Mbozi District Council (Coun­cil Health Service Board Establishment) Instrument, 2001 and shall come into operation on the date of its publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: This Instrument shall apply throughout the area of jurisdiction of Mbozi District Council .

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In the Instrument unless the context otherwise requires— " Act " means the Local Government (District Authorities) Act; "Regional Health Management Team " means a team of Health experts incharge of managing health services in the Region; Act No. 7 of 1982 " Hospital Governing Committee " means a committee established by paragraph 13 of this Instrument; " Health Management Team " means a team of head of sections or unit of Region/ District Hospital . " Health Centre Committee " means a committee established under para­graph 23 (1) of this Instrument' " Health Centre Management Team " means a team of head of section or units of a Health Centre ; " Ward Development Committee " means a committee established un­der section 31 of the Local Government (District Authorities) Act or section 15 of the Local Government (Urban Authorities) Act ; " Dispensary Committee Team " means a committee established under paragraph 30 of this Instrument' " Dispensary management Team " means a management of a dispensary; " Dispensary " means a primary Health Care facility, which offers out­patient services, including maternal and child health services, and may have up to four bed for observation of patients for not more than 24 hours; " Health Centre " means a health facility usually located at the Ward level offering outpatient and inpatient health care services including ma­ternal and child health services with a bed capacity of about 15 beds; " District Hospital " means a level I —Hospital establishment in the District empowered to supervise all health services in the district; " Designated District Hospital " means a voluntary agency level I —Hos­pital establishment empowered to act as the district hospital on con­tractual agreement with the Government; " Health Facility " means and includes a hospital, health centre, medical and dental clinics, dispensary pharmacy, health laboratory, diagnos­tic center, maternity home and nursing home and nursing home and includes such other facilities established or organized for purposes of delivering health services; " District Medical Officer " means a medical practitioner who is the of­ficer in charge of health services in the district; " Public Health Facility " means a health facility that is owned and man­aged by the government to address people's needs; " Non government Organization (NGO) health facility " means a health facility owned, financed and run by an organization or an Association which is normally organized charitable non profit Making private ser­vices, and operates within laid down Regulations of the government; " Private health facility " means a health facility owned and managed by private individual or corporate body mainly for profit; " Board " means a Council Health Service Board established under para­graph 4(1) of this Instrument; " Council " means the District Council established under section 5 of the Local Government (District Authorities) Act 1982;

provisions.title: Establishment of the Board
provisions.content: (1) There is hereby established a Board , to be known as the Mbozi District Council Health Service Board . (2) The offices of the Board shall be within the District.

provisions.title: Composition of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall be composed of two categories of members as provided hereunder:— (i) Vote members (selected members) (a) four community (Service users; (b) Three community facility representative from Hospital governing Committee, Health Centre Committee , Dispensary Committee; (c) a representative of Voluntary Agency Private not for profit; (d) one member from the Association of Private for Profit; (e) a representative of other Non governmental Association. (ii) Representation (Non Vote Councilors) (a) four members from Council : Heads of social Services Committee, Council Education Officer, Council Planning Officer, District Medical Officer and Council Water Engineer; (b) one member from District Hospital ; (c) a representative from the Regional Health Management Team . (2) Composition of the Mbozi District Council Health Services Board shall be as provided hereunder:— (a) Chairperson shall be voted from the selected members: (b) The selected members are vote members; (c) District Medical Officer shall be the Secretary to the Board . (3) The Composition-of the Board must ensure a gender balance.

provisions.title: Terms of office
provisions.content: (1) Member of the Board shall hold office for a period of three years from the date of their nomination and cease to be a member after that period, but, may be eligible for re-nomination. (2) At the end of the tenure of office of the current Board the council Director shall announce to Public the open vacancy which require com­petent personnel to fill them by filling special application form and submit them to the Council . (3) If any vacancy occurs in the membership of the Board by the reason of death, resignation permanent incapacity or any other reason, another person nominated shall hold office for the remaining term, pro­vided that nothing in this section shall be constructed as allowing to nominate another person if the remaining period is less than six months. (4) The person so nominated as per subsection (2) above be eligible for re-election. (5) If the Board fails to fulfill their expected obligations as stipu­lated by the relevant council By-laws, the Council shall dissolve the Board and to arrange for an immediate Selection. (6) Selection of applicants shall be made by the Council Manage­ment Team and thereafter be forwarded to the full Council through the Planning and Finance Committee.

provisions.title: Meeting of the Council Health Services
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of sub paragraph (2) of this para­graph, the Board shall ordinarily meet quarterly in every calendar year and all meeting of the Board shall be convened by the Chairman. (2) The Chairman may convene a special meeting of the Board on a request in writing signed by not less than one third of the total number of members of the Board . (3) The Board shall meet quarterly following the calendar year. It may also meet for an extra ordinary meeting when the need arises.

provisions.title: Quorum and voting at
provisions.content: (1) A one-half of the total number of members in office shall constitute a quorum at any meeting of the Board . (2) A decision of the majority of members present and voting at a meeting of the Board shall be deemed to be a decision of the Board ; (3) Secret vote system shall be used in Board 's meetings. (4) Every member of the Board shall have one vote and in event of equality of votes, the Chairman of the meeting shall have a second or casting vote in addition to his deliberative vote.

provisions.title: Qualifications of members
provisions.content: (1) A person shall be qualified to be a member of the Board if:- (a) He is a Tanzania citizen; (b) For representatives of NGOs and Voluntary agency the question of citizenship shall not apply; (c) He has attained the age of twenty one years and above; (d) Applicants should have completed secondary education and above. Provide that, literate adult citizen with experience, proven abili­ties and qualities in social services may be considered. (2) All seven selected members shall not hold any political post through any political party and shall not be employee of the Council . (3) A councilor and an employee of the Council whether elected or not shall not be allowed to be a voting member of the board as the later is a lower organ of the council which is accountable to and subject to evalu­ation by it.

provisions.title: Roles and functions of the Board
provisions.content: (1) The roles, functions and responsibilities of the Board shall be the following:— (i) To ensure that the population receives appropriate and affordable, promotive, preventive' curative and rehabilitative health care serv­ices; (ii) To discuss, amend Health plans, budget and submit to the Council for approval; (iii) To receive, analyze and approve implementation reports from the council health management Team; (iv) To identify, mobilize and solicit financial resources for running council heath Services; (v) To mobilize adequate resources that will ensure effective, efficient and equitable access to Health Service in the district; (vi) To support Council Health management Team in managing and administering/Health Resources; (vii) To prepare and recommend on a council wide health plan and budget tile committees responsible for health services; (viii) To promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable logis­tics and supply systems; (ix) To develop^ supervise and manage health service in the district.

provisions.title: Relationship of the Board with community, stakeholders and other authorities
provisions.content: Relationship of the Board with community, stakeholders and other Authorities shall be as follows:— (a) The Board shall ensure full involvement of the local community in priority, identification, planning, financial contribution, monitor­ing and evaluation of health services delivery; (b) Facility Committee shall be represented in the Board ; (c) The Board shall be accountable to the Council :— (i) The Council Health Plans, quarterly annual technical and finan­cial reports shall be reviewed by social services Committee and Planning committee and approved by foil council; (ii) The Board shall conduct its activities in such a way that it shall maximize the efficiency of the council and its existing organs without Council jeopardizing the Board 's autonomy. (d) The council health management Team shall be a technical structure for implementing the decisions passed by the Board ; (e) The composition of finance and management Committee shall be multi-sectoral: (i) Through this committee, all plans and progress reports pass, therefore, this committee will facilitate collaboration; (ii) Close collaboration with other sectors will be actively searcher by the Board towards the implementation of joint efforts in pri­ority areas within the district. (f) With Non Governmental Organizations, Private Providers and Donors: (i) Members representing the Voluntary Agency, Non Governmen­tal organizations and Private Providers in the Board will facili­tate relationship; (ii) The Board will require a proof from Council Health Manage­ment Team that other stakeholders have been involved in the planning process; (iii) The Board shall solicit for funds from all various source in­cluding Councils, Voluntary Agency, Community and Donors. (g) Regional Health Management Team shall provide technical support and advisory role; (h) The role of the Ministries of Regional Administration and Local government and Health shall be to formulate policies, regulatory framework and to Monitor the activities of the Council health Serv­ice Board .

provisions.title: Sources of funds
provisions.content: (1) The sources of funds for the Board shall be:— (a) community contribution; (b) community Health Fund; (c) cost sharing and user charges fees-from the facility; (d) donors and partners; (e) central Government grants; (f) other sources. (2) There shall be opened and maintained a bank account in respect of health service resources in which all health service revenue managed by the Board shall be deposited and ^withdrawn. (3) The Board will maintain special account for the purposes of Com­munity health fund initiatives. The account will maintain revenues from sales of Council Health Fund cards and collection of user fees from Council Health facilities. Signatories of this special account will be as stipulated in the By-law establishing community health Fund. (4) The Council shall appoint two accountants who shall oversee health accounts under the District Medical Officer . (5) The District medical Officer shall be responsible for approval of all payments. (6) Both accounts shall be audited following prevailing Government regulation. (7) By-laws for community Health Fund shall be formed for purposes of managing CHF activities in the area of jurisdiction of the district coun­cil of Mbozi.

provisions.title: Establishment of Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: There shall be established for each hospital under the management and supervision of the Council , or designated hospital, a Hospital Gov­erning committed to be referred to as a "Committee".

provisions.title: Composition of the Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: The Hospital Governing committee shall consist of: (a) Three persons of reputable character appointed from amongst the members of the community receiving services or registered to receive services from the Hospital; (b) Three persons appointed by the Council from members of the health Centre Committee and Dispensary committee within the area of jurisdiction of the Council ; (c) One person appointed by Council from voluntary agencies providing services within the area of jurisdiction of the council.

provisions.title: Qualification of members of the Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: Member of the Hospital Governing Committee shall have the following qualifications;— (a) Be a citizen of the United Republic of Tanzania; (b) Be 25 years age or above; (c) Be standard seven level or having an equivalent academic qualification.

provisions.title: Procedure of selecting Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provision of sub-section (2) and (3) Members of the Hospital governing Committee shall be selected in accordance with Principles of meritocracy and transparency. (2) The position of membership to the Hospital Governing Committee shall be advertised in the newspaper circulating in the area of jurisdiction of the Council , and the advertisement shall call upon qualified person to submit application to the Council Director. (3) The Board shall shortlist the names of all qualified applicants and causes the short-listed applicants to be interviewed, and submit propos­als to the Council through the Council Director of the most suitable applicants to be members of the Hospital Governing committee.

provisions.title: Tenure of office
provisions.content: The Tenure of office of a member of the Hospital governing Committee shall be three years and at the end of that period a Member shall be eligible for re-appointment.

provisions.title: Meeting of the Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Hospital governing Committee shall hold ordinary meet­ing for the transaction of its business at least once in every month at places and dates and such times as the Committee may decide. (2) The chairman of the Hospital Governing committee may after con­sultation with the Secretary of the Committee convene a special meeting on such time as it may deem necessary. (3) Notice of every meeting shall be sent to the members not less than two days before the date appointed for the meeting, notifying members in. writing of the place, date and time of the meeting and of the business proposed to be transacted at the meeting.

provisions.title: Functions of a Hospital Governing Committee
provisions.content: The functions, roles and responsibilities of the Hospital Govern­ing Committee shall be: (a) to oversee proper management of resources of the Hospital; (b) receive, discuss and pass proposals for hospital plans and budgets and submit to the council for approval through Council health Service board; (c) receive and discuss the implementation reports prepared by Hospi­tal Management Team; (d) identify mobilized and solicit financial resources for the financing of the hospital services; (e) Liaise with other health committees and partners in health provi­sion and promotion; (f) to make recommendations on selection, employment training and deployment to the relevant body of the council so as to ensure proper, effective and efficient human resource, disposition of the hospital. (g) provide feed back to the community on hospital operations and development plans.

provisions.title: Reporting and accountability
provisions.content: The Hospital management committee shall report to and be answerable and accountable to the Council through the Council Health Service Board .

provisions.title: Relationship Hospital Governing Committee with other committee or authorities
provisions.content: The Hospital Governing committee shall, for the purposes of better management, liaise with other health committees, providers for purposes of creating an integrated health services delivery within the es­tablishment system of health services in the council.

provisions.title: Sources of funds
provisions.content: The sources of revenue for the Hospital governing committee shall Consist of:— (i) such amounts that will be allocated to the hospital in the council’s budget and appropriated by the Council by way of grant through Council Health Service Board ; (ii) Health service user charges or part of such charges assigned by Council Health Service Board to form part of resources of the hospital; (iii) Such amount which may be received by way of grant or subven­tion, donation from the Council , Central government institutions or private individuals; (iv) Any other monies that may lawfully be received by the Board from any other source not specified in this Instrument.

provisions.title: Establishment of a Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) There shall be established for each Health Centre , a Health Centre Committee which shall be composed of:— (a) Members appointed one each from a ward within the area; (b) Two members appointed by the District Council ; (c) One member appointed by Non governmental Organizations providing health services in the area; (d) The officer in-charge of the Health Centre who shall be the Secretary; (e) Three persons of reputable character appointed from amongst the members of the community receiving services or registered to receive services from the Health Centre . (2) Health Centre Committee may invite other persons who are not Members of the Health Centre Committee to provide expertise and other assistance but such invited persons shall not take part in the final deci­sions of the committee.

provisions.title: Qualifications for membership
provisions.content: A person shall be qualified for appointment as a members of Health Centre Committee if: (a) He is a citizen of United Republic; (b) He has attained the age of twenty one years and above; (c) He is ordinarily resident within the area of jurisdiction of the Council ; (d) He can read and write in Kiswahili; (e) He does not hold any political post.

provisions.title: Tenure of officer of members
provisions.content: (1) The term of office of a member shall be three years and all of them shall retire on the third anniversary of the date on which they took their offices and if not re-appointed, their places shall be filled by newly appointed members who shall come in office on that day. (2) Any appointed member may resign by giving a written request to that effect signed by not less than one third of all the members of the committee stating clearly the purposes for which the special meeting is to be convened. (3) Where a member resigns or dies the vacant post shall be filled and the appointed member shall hold office only for the remainder of the term of his predecessor but shall be eligible for re-appointment.

provisions.title: Meetings of Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Committee shall hold ordinary meetings for the transac­tion of its business at least once in every three months at a place, dates and such times as it may decide. (2) The Chairman of the Committee may convene a special meeting of the Committee after he receives a written request to that effect signed by not less than one third of all the members of the committee stating clearly the purposes for which the special meeting is to be convened. (3) The Secretary shall, not less than twenty four hour before the time Appointed for the holding of a meeting of a committee, notify every mem­ber in writing of the place, day and time of the meeting and of the busi­ness proposed to be transacted at the meeting.

provisions.title: Functions of a Health Centre Committee
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of every Committee of a Health Centre : (a) to receive and discuss implementation reports prepared by the Health Centre Management Team ; (b) to identify and solicit financial resources for the running of Health Centre services; (c) to liaise with the Council Health Service Board and other partners in health provision and promotion in its area; (d) to promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply of drugs and logistic systems; (e) to advise and recommend to the Council Health Service board on human resources development in terms of recruitment, training deployment and motivation at the Health Centre level; (f) to facilitate the Health Centre Management Team in planning and managing Community based health initiatives within its catchment area in the Context of the Ward Development Plan; (g) to promote availability of adequate Health Centre infrastructure; (h) to oversee the availability of essential supplies of drugs, reagents and Medical equipment. 2. The Health Centre Committee shall be responsible and accountable for its day to day functions to the Council Health Service Board .

provisions.title: Funds and sources of revenue
provisions.content: (1) Funds and sources of revenue of a Health Centre shall consti­tute the following:— (a) Community contributions; (b) Donor or Partner donations; (c) Government Grants; (d) District Council Grants; (e) Any Other source legally acquired. (2) There shall be a special account at a Bank recognized by the Coun­cil where all the funds for a Health Centre shall be deposited. (3) There shall be two signatories for cheque of a Health Centre . The Office in-charge of a Health Centre shall be Category "A" and user of a Health Centre shall be category "B". (4) There shall be in every health Centre a strong room and a safe for the custodian of money for the Centre. (5) The Health Centre account shall be audited in accordance with the Local government financial regulations. (6) The health Centre shall send quarterly, biannual and annual progress reports to the Council Health Service Board to be represented before the full council meetings.

provisions.title: Relations with other sectors
provisions.content: (1) The Health Centre Committee shall, in performance of its Functions; (i) Make sure that the community needs are addressed; (ii) Solicit funds from various sources; (iii) Keep and maintain a Health Fund Contributor's register; (iv) Develop and strengthen partnership for collaboration; (v) Make sure that the Dispensary Committee is represented in the Health Centre Committee ; (vi) Make sure that the health Centre committee is represented in the Health Centre Committee . (2) The Council Health Management team shall give technical advice to the health Centre Management Team.

provisions.title: Establishment of Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: There is established a committee to be known as a Dispensary Committee.

provisions.title: Composition of a Dispensary Committee
provisions.content: Dispensary Committee shall be composed of the following mem­bers: (a) Selected member who shall be vote members as follows:— (i) Three members from community dispensary users; (ii) One representative from a Non-governmental association; (b) Representing non-vote members as follows:— (i) One representative from a Ward Development Committee ; (ii) One representative from a Village Government Committee; and (iii) In-Charge of the dispensary who shall be the Secretary.

provisions.title: Mode of selection and tenure of office
provisions.content: (1) Members of a Dispensary Committee shall be selected based on the following procedure:— (i) ward Executive Officer shall advertise the vacant post for represen­tation of health facilities users; (ii) Individual application shall be forwarded to the Ward Manage­ment Team for scrutiny before being forwarded to Ward Develop­ment Committee which shall do the final Selection; (iii) The Final selection shall then be forwarded to the Council for approval and inform accordingly the Ward Development Committee ; (iv) Identify and solicit financial resources for running dispensary services; (v) Liaise with other health boards and partners in health provi­sion and promotion; (vi) Promote sustainable health infrastructure and reliable supply and logistic system; (vii) Advice and recommend on human resources concerning Recruit­ment, training, selection, and deployment to relevant authorities; (viii) Assist and facilitate the Dispensary Management team in plan­ning and managing the community based health initiatives within its catchment area in the context of the Ward Development Plan; (ix) Submit quarterly, biannual and annual reports to the Ward Devel­opment Committee.

provisions.title: Relationship of Dispensary Committee with relevant
provisions.content: For the better performance and efficiency in its day to day activi­ties; a Dispensary Committee shall liaise with the following:— (i) Community by ensuring that the community contribute members to its Committee to ensure that community needs are adequately ad­dressed to facilitate flow of information from both ways; (ii) Council Health management team shall give technical advice to it; (iii) The Council Health Service Board by being represented in it on a rotational basis to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (iv) The Dispensary Management Team shall ensure that the Team implements Council 's decisions; (v) The Health Centre Committee by being represented in order to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (vi) Other stakeholders by soliciting funds, develop and strengthen partnerships for collaboration, co-ordination and integration.

provisions.title: Source of funds
provisions.content: Source of fund for a dispensary shall include but not limited to the following:— (i) Community contribution; (ii) CHF, user fees or cost sharing at the case may be and if so estab­lished; (iii) The Council Health Service Board by being represented in it on a rotational basis to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (iv) The District management Team shall ensure that the Team imple­ments council's decisions; (v) The Health Centre committee by being represented in order to ensure that dispensary concerns are addressed; (vi) Other stakeholders by soliciting funds develop and strengthen partnerships for collaboration, co-ordination and integration.

provisions.title: Source of funds
provisions.content: Source of fund for a dispensary shall include but not limited to the following:— (i) Community Contribution; (ii) CHF, user fees or cost sharing as the case may be and if so established; (iii) Council 's funds; (iv) Funds from donors or partners; (v) Central Government, and (vi) Other sources legally acquired.
